title: The Recurrent Cell
concept: A Vanilla RNN contains a loop, reusing the same set of weights to process each token of a sequence step-by-step.

exercises.prompt: What is the core structure of a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) cell?

options.text: A straight line with no memory

exercises.explain: A Vanilla RNN uses a loop to process sequence data like text, audio, or time series step-by-step.

cards.title: Reading Word by Word
cards.body: How do our brains read? We don't read all words at once. We read word-by-word, carrying the meaning of the previous words in our minds. Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) do the exact same thing using a single repeating loop!

cards.title: The Recurrent Cell
cards.body: A Recurrent Cell is the building block of an RNN. Instead of having separate parts for each word, it is a single cell that runs in a loop. It processes one word, updates its internal memory (hidden state), and then uses that exact same cell to process the next word.

cards.title: The Single Worker
cards.body: Imagine a factory worker standing in front of a conveyor belt. Instead of using a different worker for every box that rolls by, the same worker inspects every single box one by one, using their memory of the previous box to help understand the current one.

cards.title: Processing a Sentence
cards.body: If you feed the sentence 'Cats are cute' into an RNN, the recurrent cell first processes 'Cats' and makes a memory. It then loops back to process 'are' combining it with the memory of 'Cats'. Finally, it loops again to process 'cute' with the updated memory.

cards.title: The Size Myth
cards.body: Many believe that longer sentences require a larger RNN with more weights. In reality, the RNN cell stays the exact same size because it reuses its weights over and over, no matter how long the sequence is!

cards.title: Visualizing the Loop
cards.body: Standard networks use fresh parts for every input, while Recurrent cells recycle the same hardware over time.

right.text: One single cell. Loops over inputs step-by-step, carrying memory forward.

left.text: Separate layers for each input. No loops. No memory of the past.

exercises.prompt: What makes a recurrent cell different from a standard feed-forward layer?

options.text: It processes tokens step-by-step using a loop and shared weights.
It processes all tokens in parallel using different weights for each.
It ignores past tokens and only looks at the current one.
It creates a brand new neural network for each word.

exercises.explain: By looping and using the same weights for every token, the network can handle text of any length without needing a massive model.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A recurrent cell keeps track of past information by passing a memory state to the next step of the loop.
exercises.explain: This is true! The loop in the RNN allows it to pass a 'hidden state' (memory) from one step to the next.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word about how RNNs process sequences:
exercises.sentence: An RNN cell processes each step of a sequence by reusing the same set of ____________.
exercises.explain: To keep things efficient, the recurrent cell reuses the exact same weights (parameters) at every step of the loop.

exercises.prompt: Which system describes a true Recurrent Cell processing a sequence?

options.text: System A: Processes 'Cats', then processes 'are' separately without using the memory of 'Cats'.
System B: Processes 'Cats', updates its memory, and passes that memory to the same cell to process 'are'.
System C: Uses three different neural networks to process three words at the same time.

exercises.explain: System B is the AI because a recurrent cell must pass its previous memory (hidden state) to the next step to understand the context of the sequence. System A doesn't pass any information forward, and System C doesn't reuse the cell.

exercises.prompt: How does a Vanilla RNN cell process a sequence of text?

options.text: It uses a completely new set of weights for each word in the sentence.
It uses a single loop, applying the same weights to each word and passing its memory forward.
It processes all words at the exact same time without keeping any memory.
It forgets everything it saw in the previous step to save computer memory.

exercises.explain: A recurrent cell processes the sequence step-by-step using a loop, passing its hidden state (memory) forward while keeping the internal weights identical for every step!
